# Lease-document generation as external I/O (design)

**Status: 🏗️ building** — direction Andrew-steered 2026-07-08 ("lease docs are generated by an
external jurisdiction-aware service — like Stripe for payments; bridge + Loom externalTask + async
external-reply; a sanctioned engine actor attaches; the app only displays") and the build instructed
2026-07-09 ("Phase 2 — moving the lease-document generation to external I/O, mirroring the existing
lease-signing service pattern"). Residuals flagged in §8 for Andrew's review; no contract change
(§7). Parent item: **#75 Fire 2b** (`backlog/lattice.md` → real-actor-write-auth-e2e), the
forgery-closure whose increments 1+2 (clinic/cafe strip `f8dec9c`, loftspace object-attach
browser-direct `ed90925`) already shipped.

## 1. Problem

`cmd/loftspace-app/lease_document.go` renders the executed-lease document **in the app** and
self-submits `AttachObject` as its admin actor over `ops.>` (`handleLeaseDocumentAttach`,
`lease_document.go:125-200`). NATS transport permissions are subject-based and cannot inspect the
envelope actor, so "app can publish core-operations" always means "a compromised app can forge any
actor." This is the **last** vertical-app direct `core-operations` publish —
`internal/natsperm/conf_test.go:293-296` explicitly names this flow as the reason loftspace-app is
not yet in `TestVerticalAppOpsPublishDenied`.

It is also modeled wrong: a lease document is jurisdiction-aware legal content produced by an
**external vendor** (Rocket Lawyer / Anvil / PandaDoc analog) — external I/O, not app code. The
platform already has the exact machinery for that: the `lease-signing` externalTask triad
(Contract #10 §10.5/§10.6, `docs/components/bridge.md`).

## 2. Shape — all package data + one adapter + deploy grants

Zero engine changes. Zero frozen-contract changes. The flow, end to end:

```
SignLease commits (.signature aspect)
  → lens leaseApplicationComplete: gap missing_leaseDoc
  → Weaver playbook: triggerLoom{pattern: leaseDocument, subject: row.entityKey}
  → Loom externalTask: submits CreateLeaseDocInstance (claim vtx.service.<handle>,
      class service.docGen.instance; assembles doc fields Processor-side; emits external.docGen)
      … parks on token.<handle>
  → bridge dispatches adapter "docGen" (the mock external legal-doc vendor):
      renders the document, ObjectPut → core-objects (bytes inert until anchored),
      returns Resolved{completed, Detail:{digest,size,contentType,storeName,filename}}
  → bridge posts RecordLeaseDocOutcome (deterministic requestId):
      create-only .outcome aspect on the claim + orchestration.externalTaskCompleted → Loom advances
  → lens reprojects: gap missing_leaseDocAttach (outcome completed ∧ signedLease link absent)
  → Weaver playbook: directOp AttachObject{digest,size,contentType,storeName,filename
      ← row columns; targetKey=row.entityKey; linkName="signedLease"}, reads:[row.entityKey]
  → objects-base AttachObject commits vtx.object.<oid> + the signedLease link → gap clears
  → loftspace-app DISPLAYS the attached artifact (P5 read + ObjectGet stream) — no op submission
```

Two Weaver hops instead of one bespoke mechanism, because the platform's own guardrails require it
(§3 "why Weaver attaches").

## 3. Decisions + grounding

**D1 — subject is the `leaseapp`, not the applicant identity.** The document is *about* the
application; the claim's `providedTo` and the attach target are the leaseapp; the gap lives on
`leaseApplicationComplete` whose `row.entityKey` is the leaseapp key (the `missing_signature`
precedent, `packages/lease-signing/targets.go:38-43`).

**D2 — new instanceOp/replyOp; reuse `RecordServiceDispatch` + its marker gate.**
`CreateLeaseServiceInstance` is identity-subject-shaped and constrains `family ∈ {backgroundCheck,
payment}` (`scripts.go:711-728`); `RecordLeaseServiceOutcome`'s `.outcome` schema is
`{status, completedAt, validUntil}` and keeps the free-form result **off** the aspect — but the doc
pointers must live **on** the aspect (the lens + the attach playbook read them). So:
`CreateLeaseDocInstance` (family `docGen`, subject leaseapp) and `RecordLeaseDocOutcome`
(`.outcome` = `{status, completedAt, digest, size, contentType, storeName, filename}`, aspect class
`leaseDocOutcome` with its own exact-class gate DDL — the `leaseServiceOutcome` gate rationale,
`ddls.go:446-455`, applies verbatim). The async pending marker reuses `RecordServiceDispatch` +
`leaseServiceDispatchMarker` unchanged (both reconstruct `vtx.service.<handle>` generically), so a
future real async vendor rides the shipped §10.4 poll/timeout lane with no package change; the
reference adapter is sync (`FakeBackgroundCheck` shape) and never triggers it.

**D3 — doc fields are assembled Processor-side by the instanceOp (the ratified write-path read).**
Contract §10.5: params templates cover subject aspects; "a field on a *linked* vertex is reached by
the instanceOp DDL's own §2.5 `kv.Read`". Loom hydrates the subject root
(`inferExternalTaskReads`, `internal/loom/externaltask_params.go:36-80`); the script reads the
leaseapp's `.terms`/`.signature` via `kv.Read`, walks `kv.Links(leaseapp, "applicationFor"/"appliesToUnit",
"out")` (§2.5.1, `internal/processor/starlark_kv.go:108-187`) to the applicant + unit, and
`kv.Read`s their fields (identity profile name; unit address/city/region/rent/currency/bedrooms/
bathrooms/leaseTermMonths/availableFrom). The emitted `external.docGen` params carry the resolved
doc fields — the adapter (vendor) receives real fields and never touches our graph or a lens
(exactly the contract's "vendor's legal-name / DOB / address" case). Unsigned subject → the script
fails the op (no dispatch); absent optional fields degrade exactly as the current renderer does;
an applicant with no profile name renders by bare key (same rule as today — the name now comes
from Core KV identity state, the write-path source of truth, not the roster read-model).

**D4 — the adapter does the byte-plane write; the bridge gains `$O.core-objects.>`.** Bytes cannot
ride the outcome op (blobs stay off-graph — `large-file-binary-design.md`), and the bridge holds no
object grant today (`deploy/gen-dev-nkeys/main.go:159-165`). The `docGen` adapter is constructed in
`cmd/bridge` with a substrate conn + the core-objects bucket + upload cap (cmd/bridge already holds
a conn; adapters are just registry entries — `internal/bridge/adapter.go:143-181`). It renders,
`ObjectPut`s under the deterministic store name `DeriveNanoID("loftspace:lease-doc:store:",
leaseAppKey)` — the SAME namespace the app uses today, so a re-render overwrites the same store
object and never leaks an orphan — and dedups on `idempotencyKey` (per-adapter contract,
`adapter.go:99-107`). The bridge becomes the **4th** sanctioned object-plane writer (pinned in
`TestObjectStoreWriteAccess` alongside object-store-manager/loupe/loftspace-app) — strictly less
power than the ops-submission it already holds; bytes are inert until an op anchors them.

**D5 — Weaver attaches (the sanctioned engine actor), via generic `directOp AttachObject`.** The
replyOp cannot mint the anchor itself: step-6 class→DDL resolution routes an `object`-class
mutation to objects-base's DDL, whose `PermittedCommands` is `{AttachObject, DetachObject,
TombstoneObject}` — a lease-signing op writing object vertices fails closed (the platform's own
write gate forbids the shortcut). The bridge posts exactly one replyOp (its generic shape,
`bridge.md` In/Out). So the anchor is a genuine `AttachObject`, dispatched by **Weaver** off the
`missing_leaseDocAttach` gap with the attach payload drawn from projected row columns
(`directOp` params are `row.<column>`-templated + type-preserving, Contract §10.8; the
`SetListingStatus` row is the precedent, `targets.go:41-43` + `reads:["row.entityKey"]` per
14.6 reads-on-dispatch). Weaver's system actor holds `operator` → `AttachObject` is granted
(`packages/objects-base/permissions.go:16-31`); the actuator's deterministic dispatch requestId
dedups re-fires; a cleared link clears the gap so convergence terminates. Self-healing for free: a
lost/detached executed lease re-attaches; nothing new to operate.

**D6 — the app only displays.** Delete `handleLeaseDocumentAttach` + `renderLeaseDocument` + the
`SubmitOp` path + the FE `ensureLeaseDocument` hook (`web/app.js:1709,1914-1924` — the platform
now converges the document automatically on signing; no client action). GET
`/api/lease-document` keeps its URL + RLS boundary (authenticate → applicant-self row via the
protected `leaseApplicationsRead` model) but serves the **attached artifact's bytes** (doc
pointers → `ObjectGet` stream) instead of re-rendering — one renderer in the system (the vendor),
zero drift. The protected lens projects the doc-pointer columns for that read. A GET inside the
convergence window (signed, doc not yet attached) returns 404 "document is being generated" — the
honest async answer; the FE anchor already tolerates a 404.

## 4. Failure + idempotency story (mirrors the shipped invariants)

- Redelivered `external.docGen` → adapter memo-dedup on `instanceKey` + deterministic
  `deriveReplyRequestID(instanceKey)` collapse on the Contract #4 tracker; create-only `.outcome`
  is the once-only backstop (FR58 chain unchanged).
- Weaver re-fires of `triggerLoom` while a claim is in flight are suppressed lens-side (the
  `inflight_*` column pattern, `lenses.go:577-578`); a completed outcome ends the gap.
- Adapter render failure / a future async vendor's give-up timeout → terminal `.outcome
  status=failed` → `declined_docGen`-style surfacing; **no auto-retry** (mirrors the bgcheck
  decline posture; §8-R1).
- Attach keeps failing → bytes reclaimed by object-store-manager's never-attached backstop after
  its grace window; the gap persists loudly (Health/console), no silent wedge.
- Everything downstream of `SignLease` is convergence-driven — a crashed bridge/Loom/Weaver resumes
  from durable consumers; no app-side orchestration remains.

## 5. The enforcement payoff (what this fire is FOR)

With no op submission left in loftspace-app: remove its `ops.>` publish in
`deploy/gen-dev-nkeys` (keep `$O.core-objects.>` byte-ingest + reads; keys reused, conf
regenerated) and add loftspace-app to `TestVerticalAppOpsPublishDenied` — **all three vertical
apps are then transport-denied from core-operations**, closing #75 Fire 2b. The Gateway
(`/v1/operations`, Bearer-auth, strip-client-actor) is the only write door for apps.

## 6. Build plan (one fire, internal order)

1. `packages/lease-signing`: `CreateLeaseDocInstance` + `RecordLeaseDocOutcome` + `leaseDocOutcome`
   aspect-gate DDLs (+ scripts), `leaseDocument` Loom pattern, lens columns (docGen fan-out +
   `missing_leaseDoc` / `inflight` / `missing_leaseDocAttach` + doc-pointer columns + protected-lens
   doc pointers), playbook rows, operator grants + OpMetas. Colocated tests (mirror
   `lease_signing_test.go` / `renewal_ops_test.go` patterns).
2. `internal/bridge`: `docGen` adapter (deterministic renderer ported from
   `cmd/loftspace-app/lease_document.go` + ObjectPut + memo dedup) + tests; register in
   `cmd/bridge` with conn/bucket/cap.
3. `cmd/loftspace-app`: delete POST/renderer/SubmitOp path; GET → attached-bytes streaming; FE hook
   removal.
4. `deploy/gen-dev-nkeys` + regenerated `deploy/nats-server.conf`; `internal/natsperm/conf_test.go`
   (ops-denied + object-writers matrices).
5. Docs same-commit: `docs/components/bridge.md` adapter list + object-plane grant note.
6. Gates: `go build ./...`, `make vet`, `golangci-lint`, `make verify-kernel`, full `go test ./...`
   (wide-change rule), `verify-package` for lease-signing; live e2e on the shared capability-mode
   stack (sign → auto-generated → attached → displayed; loftspace-app ops.> denied).

## 7. Contract surface — none

§10.5/§10.6 externalTask, §10.4 scheduling, §10.8 playbook actions, Contract #4 dedup, objects-base
AttachObject: all used as frozen/shipped. New ops/aspects/patterns/lens/playbook = package data
(Decision #10). Deploy grants = dev config pinned by natsperm tests. `docs/components/bridge.md` is
a component page (same-commit update rule), not a contract.

## 8. Residuals (flagged for Andrew)

- **R1 — failed render is terminal (no auto-retry).** Mirrors the bgcheck decline posture. A
  transient vendor failure therefore needs a manual re-trigger today (fresh `StartLoomPattern`).
  If that bites, a retry budget on technical-`failed` (the `retry_budget.go` precedent) is the
  follow-on — deliberately not built speculatively.
- **R2 — bridge as 4th object-plane writer.** Posture call: trusted-infra egress gains byte-write.
  Pinned in `TestObjectStoreWriteAccess`; revisit if the object plane ever splits per-tenant.
- **R3 — brief GET 404 window while the document converges** (was: synchronous availability on
  first click). Honest async; surfaced as "being generated".
- **R4 — the executed-lease renderer now lives in the reference vendor adapter** (the mock of the
  external service). When a real vendor integration lands (Phase 3 per `bridge.md`), the fake and
  its renderer stay as the CI/e2e reference, same as `FakeStripe`.
